meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Mastering Debugging: Project Management for Student AI Builders
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how structured project management and tools like Antigravity and Claude Code can transform your student coding workflow.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of process. By implementing structured project management, you break complex logic into trackable units. Combine this with AI-assisted debugging tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to isolate errors, document state changes, and maintain momentum while building your first production-ready SaaS application.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your terminal is a graveyard of stack traces. You feel the familiar weight of 'can't debug code'—that paralyzing moment where logic dissolves into frustration. You aren't failing; you are simply hitting the wall that separates hobbyists from professional engineers. Debugging is not an innate gift; it is a discipline forged in the fires of systematic project management.

Imagine a workflow where every bug is a documented milestone rather than a blockade. By treating your code like an architectural project, you stop guessing and start engineering. With modern AI agents as your copilots, you can turn these roadblocks into your greatest learning opportunities, building the resilience required to scale from a student project to a real-world SaaS.

steps.title: Define the Atomic Problem
steps.description: Before fixing, isolate the failure. Break your feature into the smallest possible functional unit to identify where the logic breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this code block and identify the specific function or logic gate causing the runtime exception. Focus on input validation.

steps.title: Document State Transitions
steps.description: Track how data changes across functions. Use logging to create a paper trail that reveals the exact moment your variable state drifts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive logging strategy for this class to track state changes during asynchronous execution.

steps.title: Implement Defensive Unit Tests
steps.description: Create tests that explicitly fail when the bug occurs. This ensures that when you fix it, the bug stays dead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a PyTest suite that targets the identified edge case and currently triggers a failure.

steps.title: Systematic Refactoring
steps.description: Rewrite the broken logic using clean code principles. Simplify complex nesting to make the error impossible to replicate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function to be more modular and readable, removing deep nesting that hides the bug.

steps.title: Final Verification & CI/CD
steps.description: Automate the check to ensure your fix integrates perfectly with the rest of your codebase without regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the final code for potential security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks post-fix.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'Fix: Resolved state drift in auth module'

pro_tips: Treat every bug report as a feature request for better error handling.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude Code; explain your logic out loud before asking for a fix.
Never fix a bug without writing a regression test first; if you can't test it, you haven't fixed it.
Version control is your undo button; commit early and often to create 'save points' in your logic.
Focus on the 'Why' of the error, not just the 'What'; understanding the root cause prevents recurrence.
content.next_steps: Once you've cleared your current blockers, document your fix in a project README. This turns your debugging struggle into a professional portfolio piece that demonstrates your ability to troubleshoot complex systems.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the process to stop struggling and start scaling. Don't let a single error stop your momentum—you are building the future of software, one commit at a time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
